rulings.Case Title: SHERRI WATSON VS. ROBERT G GISH M.D.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 30, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER's motion For Terminating And Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the unopposed motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SEQUOIA DUNKINSELL VS. CYPRESS SECURITY, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continued Hearing Date For Motion For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 30, 2012, Line 13. PLAINTIFF SEQUOIA DUNKINSELL'S (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continued Hearing Date For Motion For Attorneys Fees. Granted in part. Defendant must pay $15,000 as reasonable fees to plaintiff. The fees were reduced because plaintiff is not entitled to fees incurred after the 998 offer based on the express terms of the compromise, the billing entries for client and co-counsel conferences were excessive, and the plaintiff achieved very limited success. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

Pro Tem Judge James Watson, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Grant the unopposed motion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to jamesw@skwsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: HANSON PERMANENTE CEMENT, INC. F/K/A KAISER CEMENT VS. JOHN WALLACE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 30, 2012, Line 18. DEFENDANT JOHN WALLACE'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Denied. All of the claims alleged by the plaintiff arise from Defendant's acceptance of employment with Brayton Purcell adverse to defendants, and not from any protected activity. Plaintiffs' request for monetary sanctions is denied because this case is at least arguably distinguishable from United States Fire Insurance, Freeman and Benasra because of the breadth of the relief sought by plaintiffs. However, the foregoing line of cases should have, at the very least, been discussed in defendant's initial memorandum. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ELLEN SOULIS VS. JACQUES MAHUERA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jacques Manuera'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint.
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, May 30, 2012, Line 20. Defendant Jacques Manuera'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. Overruled. Plaintiff has standing to bring her claims. Manuera and Metro Café are proper defendants. The First Amended Complaint is sufficiently certain and alleges facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action for breach of contract. The error in Manuera's name is of no legal consequence.
